cooperates.cooperate_desc: This is Marcel's ultimate partner. When Marcel uses his Ultimate (Golden Hour), Beatrix can fire her Wesker (Shotgun) or Nibiru (SMG) Ultmates directly into the frozen field. All that massive damage is "stored" and hits the enemy simyltaneously the millisecond the stasis ends, resulting in an instant "one-shot" deletion.

Terizla provides the heavy crowd control Marcel needs to land his delated Skill 1. Terizla's Penalty Zone pulls enemies into a tight cluster. You can then use your high speed to dash in, freeze them all, and allow Terizla to land his slow but powerful Smashes without the enemies being able to dash away.

Odette's biggest weakness is being interrupted during her Ultimate. Marcel can "protect the stage" by freezing any incoming CC or burst damage the would stop her. Furthermore, since your build includes Fleeting Time and Temporal Reign, you can keep enemies trapped in her Swan Song radius for much longer than usual.

Nolan is a high-mobility assassin who relies on "fractures." Marcel's Skill 1 immobilization and Thunder Belt slow make it incredibly easy for Nolan to land his rift combos. Your Conceal active from your bots allows Nolan to dive the backline without being spotted by wards, creating a "silent assassin" Duo.

Similar to Beatrix, Vale can drop his entrie combo (Windstorm and Deathblow) into Marcel's stasis field. Since the enemies are frozen and cannot use Flicker or dash skill, Vale's delayed explosion is guaranteed to hit every target inside. Your high rotation speed allows you to find Vale and peel for him whenever he is dove by assassins.

counters.counter_desc: She can steal your Golden Hour. Because the stolen ultimate treats her as the "owner," she is immune to the freeze effect while inside. She can walk through your frozen teammates and execute them while they can't move.

Your passive converts extra HP into Hybrid Defense. Lesly's passive deal True Damage, which completely ignores that defense. She can "two-tap" you from long range before you can even get close enough to snap a photo.

While he can't "cleanse" the 3-second freeze of your Ultimate, he can perfectly time his Auto-Alarm Bombs and Shield to burst you or protect his team the exact millisecond the stasis ends. He nullifies your Skill 1 entirely.

Marcel has a slight "cast delay" on his skills. Franco's Suppression is the only CC that cannot be Purified. He can hook you out your team and suppress you, killing you before you can cast your Ultimate.

Martis is immune to Crowd Control during his Skill 2. He can literally walk through your Framed Moment (Skill 1) and your Ultimate's edge to reach you. Since you have low raw HP, he can easily finish you with his True Damage execute.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Laning & Rotation Precautions (Early to Mid)

Don't Overstay: With Rapid Boots and Agility, your job is to be a "ghost." If a lane is stable, leave immediately. Your value is in the fog of war.

Team Fight Strategies

The "Pre-Freeze" Setup: Before using your Ultimate, cast Skill 1 in the direction you think the enemy will run. Because of Temporal Reign, the cooldown will refresh while they are frozen, allowing you to drop a second frame immediately after the Ult ends.

Marcel's Pro Tricks
The Retribution Denier: At Lord or Turtle, don't wait for the Lord to get low. Ult the Enemy Jungler when the Lord is at 3,000 HP. They cannot use Retribution while frozen, giving your Jungler a guaranteed 3-second window to secure the objective.

emblems.emblem_desc: My Emblem set prioritizes Macro (Rotation) and Utility (Spamming CC) over raw defense, which is the best way to play him in a coordinated team.

equips.equip_desc:
This build reaches the 40% Cooldown Reduction cap very quickly. Because you have Pull Yourself Together, your Conceal active will be ready roughly every 68 seconds instead of 80, meaning you can set up ganks constantly.

Tip
If the enemy team has high HP regen or lifesteal (like Yu Zhong or Ruby), you might consider swapping Antique Cuirass for Dominance Ice to add anti-heal to your kit!

hero.hero_overview:
Marcel, the Soul Photographer, is a unique Support hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB), released on March 11, 2026. He is the 132nd hero in the game and is characterized by his elegant aesthetic and his camera companion, Clemar, which houses his split soul.

Marcel is designed as a frontline controller rather than a backline healer. His ultimate can be used strategically to nullify turret damage during dives, zone enemy junglers from objectives like Lord or Turtle, or protect teammates by freezing them during lethal enemy combos.
hero.hero_strength:
Marcel's primary strength lies in his unparalleled battlefield control and ability to manipulate teamfight flow through his "stasis" mechanics. Unlike typical supports, his power comes from stopping both enemies and incoming damage.

Best Strategic Uses:
Use the ultimate to "freeze" the enemy jungler right as the Lord or Turtle is about to be secured, preventing them from using Retribution.

 Since projectiles are suspended in his ultimate and resolve all at once when it ends, he synergizes perfectly with teammates who can stack damage into the field (like Beatrix or Vale).
hero.hero_weakness:
Despite his powerful control, Marcel has several distinct weaknesses that can be exploited by disciplined teams.

Best Counter-Picks:

Valir: His knockback and slow keep Marcel at a distance, making it hard for him to land his Skill 1 or get close enough for an Ultimate.

Diggie: While Marcel's stasis is unique, Diggie's Ultimate provides the essential shield and CC cleansing needed to reset a fight after Marcel tries to initiate.

Lunox: Her ability to become untargetable allows her to reposition or avoid the stasis field entirely while dealing massive %HP damage.

data.recommend: To master Marcel using your high-speed "Phantom Stasis Specialist" build, follow these advanced guide recommendations to maximize your efficiency in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB).

spell.spell_desc: Flicker: This is the most popular choice for Marcel. It allows you to reposition instantly for a perfect Ultimate (Golden Hour) or escape dangerous situations. You can also use it to surprise enemies with a Flicker + Skill 1 combo for an unexpected immobilization.

hero.hero_overview:
Marcel, the "Soul Photographer" of the Paxley family. Released on March 11, 2026, he is the 132nd hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang. Marcel is a unique Support/Tank who introduces the "Frozen Moment" mechanic—literally pausing time for friends and foes alike.  Marcel carries a sentient camera named Clemar. He does not just stun enemies; he freezes them in stasis. Interestingly, Marcel is a "HP-to-Defense" converter, meaning he gains tankiness from health items but scales his damage and shields through his defensive stats. 


HERO SKILLS:

📸 Passive (Platinum Snap): Marcel cannot Crit and doesn't benefit from extra Attack Speed. Instead, Extra HP is converted into 1.5% Hybrid Defense. Clemar automatically "snaps" photos of nearby enemies, dealing True Damage based on their Max HP and granting Marcel a shield.

📷 Skill 1 (Framed Moment): Clemar takes a delayed shot in an area. After a short delay, enemies inside are immobilized and take physical damage that scales with Marcel's Physical and Magic Defense.

📷 Skill 2 (Tracking Shot): A mobility skill. Marcel enters a "Tracking Haste" state, gaining movement speed that increases as he nears enemies. Recasting allows him to dash, leaving a phantom behind. 

📸 Ultimate (Golden Hour): Marcel creates a massive wide-angle stasis field. Everything inside—enemies, allies, lord, turrets, and even flying projectiles—is frozen in time. Only Marcel can move and deal damage inside.


Standard Combo:
Skill 2 (Approach) > Skill 1 (Position) > Basic Attacks > Ultimate (to secure the kill or reset the fight).
hero.hero_strength:
✨Anti-Projectile: His Ultimate can literally stop a Franco hook or a Novaria blast mid-air.

✨Insane Durability: Because his HP converts to Hybrid Defense, he becomes incredibly difficult to kill with standard penetration.

✨Objective Control: He can freeze the enemy Jungler to prevent them from using Retribution on the Lord.
hero.hero_weakness:
✨Double-Edged Sword: A poorly timed Ultimate can freeze your own teammates, ruining their big plays.  

✨Zero Burst: He relies on sustained True Damage and CC; he cannot "delete" enemies quickly.

✨Vulnerable to True Damage: Since he relies on high Defense stats rather than massive HP pools, heroes like Karrie or Gord can melt him.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Beatrix enhances Marcel's effectiveness as a teammate by leveraging his crowd control and ultimate abilities. When Marcel immobilizes enemies, Beatrix can attack safely with her high burst damage weapons, such as rockets and SMGs. Additionally, Marcel provides protection through shields and disruption, ensuring Beatrix remains safe and can consistently contribute damage during teamfights.

Change is the ideal teammate for Marcel due to his burst damage and mobility, which enhance Marcel's crowd control. When Marcel immobilizes enemies, Change can quickly execute powerful combos for target elimination. Furthermore, Change's repositioning abilities and disruption skills increase pressure in fights, making them a great duo in team engagements.

Zhuxin is a top teammate for Marcel, as her crowd control and high damage output complement his ultimate well. When Marcel freezes foes, Zhuxin can unleash her skills to inflict significant AoE damage and maintain crowd control, ensuring team safety. This synergy enables them to eliminate multiple targets quickly while protecting vital allies, establishing them as a formidable pair in teamfights.

counters.counter_desc: Lylia presents a significant challenge for Marcel due to her high mobility and long-range capabilities, which allow her to punish him before he can engage. She effectively zones and pokes him, complicating his ability to use his ultimate and skills. Additionally, her passive ability enhances her escape and repositioning, making encounters against her particularly risky for Marcel, especially during the early and mid game when he is establishing his impact.

Kaja effectively counters Marcel due to her ability to isolate targets and quickly burst them down. His Divine Judgment can disrupt Marcel's positioning, potentially preventing his setup and freezing of enemies. Furthermore, Kaja's mobility enables her to dodge or interrupt Marcel's skills, complicating his ability to control teamfights.

Valentina effectively counters Marcel by utilizing her Soul Eater skill to steal his ultimate, transforming his potent freeze into an advantage for her team. Her long-range attacks and safe harassing ability complicate Marcel's positioning for crowd control, and with precise timing, she can entirely disrupt his teamfight strategies, marking her as a significant threat to him.

dominants.dominant_desc: In the early game, Marcel emphasizes roaming to assist teammates in securing lanes instead of focusing on farming or dealing damage. His priorities include protecting his core, providing vision, and utilizing crowd control to aid in skirmishes or deter enemy aggression. With strong mobility and utility, he effectively rotates between lanes and objectives, facilitating kills and enhancing team safety during the laning phase.

Marcel becomes increasingly influential during mid game by utilizing his crowd control for initiating and disrupting enemy strategies. He should remain with the team to secure objectives like Turtle and leverage his ultimate to swing skirmishes in their favor. Improved coordination will enable him to facilitate kills more reliably and safeguard essential teammates in critical battles.

In the late game, Marcel emerges as a crucial playmaker, capable of turning the match with a well-timed ultimate. Utilizing the invisible roam item, he can move stealthily to initiate attacks and surprise multiple enemies. It is important for him to stay close to his core, offering protection and waiting for the optimal moment to engage, as a single error or mistimed skill can lead to defeat.

emblems.emblem_desc: Marcel's chosen enhancements focus on improving mobility and team utility. Agility provides him with increased movement speed, allowing for quicker rotations and better positioning for fights. Wilderness Blessing further accelerates his speed in jungle and river areas, aiding in efficient roaming and rapid response to skirmishes. Additionally, Focusing Mark amplifies the damage enemies receive after being subjected to his skills, supporting his reliance on teammates for damage and facilitating kill securing.

equips.equip_desc: This build helps him sustain in battle. Fleeting time also gives him a boost to recover his ultimate quickly.

hero.hero_overview: Marcel is a distinctive hero with the ability to freeze enemies, allies, and projectiles in time, allowing him to uniquely control battles. Marcel is a support hero who can influence battles significantly by stopping time, allowing skilled players to change the outcome of losing fights through strategic decision-making.
hero.hero_strength: Marcel excels in controlling teamfights through enemy immobilization, ally shielding, and engagement disruption, positioning him as a crucial frontline support. His ultimate ability freezes enemies in an area, enabling impactful plays and countering attacks, while his tanky nature allows him to effectively protect teammates amidst battles.
hero.hero_weakness: Marcel's primary weakness is his reliance on timing and team coordination; poor use of his skills can inadvertently disrupt his team. He inflicts low damage and requires teammates to secure kills, making good positioning essential for his effectiveness, despite his tankiness.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Start with skill 1 and go help jungler till they get Lv4 or first turtle.

Don't get cocky and do not dive towers.
Keep showing enemy positions to your best ability.

You get more usage if you fight with multiple enemies, however targetting single target may also be viable depending on enemy line up.

When teamfights will start to occur you are good initiator. However, remember not to rush to use your ultimate - it's powerful mechanism, but very tricky to land it right. Make sure that once it's casted, it will put enemy to disadvantage:
a) your teammates will be able to catch up with your position
b) you will disable some enemies from teamfight happening nearby
c) protect your teammate from getting certain death

Optimal use of ultiamte is after you jump into enemy line and activate passive, After that, you can trigger it 2nd time.
However, do not be obsessed with optimization, as it may cause you to keep Ultimate for later, but sometimes you cannot do much when "later" comes.
Once you disrupted enemies, you should either:
a) try to run after fleeing single enemy with your teammate
or
b) if you were hurt, escape, so you can heal again and get back to fight after short while

Chastise Pauldron - if enemy team mostly hurts your team with their basic attack.

This time we are trying to counter enemies' CC.
More tailored for enemies that have burst-type DMG.

hero.hero_overview: Marcel is a Support/Crowd Control hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB), often played as a tanky roamer specializing in time-manipulation, disruption, and shielding allies. Known as the "Soul Photographer," he uses his camera companion, Clemar, to freeze enemies and immobilize them. Marcel thrives on coordinating with teammates to isolate targets.
hero.hero_strength:
Strong Crowd Control
 
- His Passive - Platinum Snap automatically immobilizes nearby enemies and deals true damage based on their max HP, with basic attacks reducing the cooldown per target.
- Skill 1 - Framed Moment slows and then immobilizes enemies in an area, great for setup and zoning.
- Ultimate - Golden Hour freezes all units and projectiles in a large area for 3 seconds, putting enemies in stasis while allowing Marcel to act freely and reset his passive cooldown for affected targets.
 
Excellent Team Support
 
- The passive grants shields to Marcel and a nearby ally when triggered, enhancing frontline durability.
- Skill 2 - Tracking Shot provides movement speed (boosted when moving toward enemies) and a dash for repositioning or engagement; if Skill 1 is active, Clemar stays to maintain the attack area.
 
Unique Scaling & Durability
 
- Damage scales with physical attack, physical defense, and magic defense, so building defensively doesn't sacrifice damage output.
- Extra HP converts to hybrid defense, improving both physical and magic durability, and he has strong natural HP regeneration.
 
Strategic Disruption
 
- His time-manipulation mechanics can completely halt enemy momentum, disrupt their skill rotations and positioning, and create opportunities for the team to secure kills or objectives.
hero.hero_weakness:
Early-Game Vulnerability
 
- He has low HP in the early game, making him an easy target for enemies. Proper positioning and protection from teammates are crucial during this phase to avoid being eliminated prematurely.
 
Ultimate Limitations
 
- His ultimate "Golden Hour" has a short delay, allowing mobile heroes like Hayabusa or Lancelot to dodge it. Additionally, the stasis effect applies to both enemies and allies, so mistiming it can inadvertently protect opponents. Moreover, heroes with suppression mechanics (e.g., Kaja's "Divine Judgment") can bypass the ultimate's invulnerability and continue to affect Marcel during its duration.
 
Lack of Self-Sustain
 
- Unlike many other support heroes, Marcel does not have any built-in HP regeneration skills or passives. He relies on battle spells like "Regen" or item effects for healing, which have cooldowns and may not be sufficient in critical situations.
 
Low Damage Output
 
- Marcel is primarily a support/tank hero and is not designed to be a damage dealer. His damage is limited, so he cannot effectively carry fights or take down enemies on his own. Overly aggressive playstyles that focus on dealing damage instead of supporting the team will likely be ineffective.
 
Susceptibility to Certain Heroes
 
- He struggles against heroes with targeted or continuous skills, such as Hayabusa, Odette, Chou, Lunox, and Aamon. Additionally, long-range burst damage heroes like Lesley and sustained magic damage dealers like Lunox can pressure him from a safe distance, making it difficult for him to utilize his kit effectively. Heroes with instant crowd control, like Eudora, can also lock him down before he can react.
 
Team Coordination Dependence
 
- His effectiveness heavily relies on team coordination. Using his ultimate requires precise timing and communication to ensure that allies can capitalize on the stasis effect while avoiding friendly fire. without proper teamwork,his impact on team fight can be significantly reduced.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Julian is countered by heroes with heavy suppressive crowd control or those who can burst him down before his lifesteal and Queen's Wings kick in. High-mobility marksmen or mages who can kite his relatively short-range skills can also be a major threat. Additionally, heroes that naturally build anti-heal (Life Drain) items significantly reduce the effectiveness of his spell vamp and the healing bonus from Oracle, making him much easier to take down during team fights.

counters.counter_desc: Carmilla is countered by high-burst mages and marksmen who can eliminate her before she gets close enough to link enemies with her ultimate. Heroes with long-range displacement or suppressive crowd control can also prevent her from successfully initiating. Additionally, since she relies on her passive to steal defenses and healing from Oracle to stay alive, enemies who build anti-heal items like Necklace of Durance or Sea Halberd significantly weaken her survivability in team fights.

dominants.dominant_desc: During the early and mid-game, utilize your movement speed to roam through the river and provide vision for your teammates. Look for opportunities to dive the backline with your Ultimate, as Fleeting Time allows you to cast it more frequently. In team fights, prioritize absorbing high-damage skills when your HP is low to trigger Queen's Wings and Oracle for maximum survival. Use the Dire Hit blessing to help secure kills on escaping enemies, and always be the first to rotate to Turtle or Lord objectives to maintain map control.

emblems.emblem_desc: This emblem set is chosen to transform a durable tank into a high-speed disruptor by prioritizing map mobility and burst damage scaled to your health. By combining Agility and Wilderness Blessing, you gain a massive movement speed advantage, particularly in the river, allowing you to rotate between lanes faster than your opponents to set up ganks or provide backup. This is paired with Concussive Blast, which uses your high HP pool to deal area-of-effect magic damage (100 + 7\% \text{ Total HP}), significantly improving your wave clear and providing extra punch during team fights. Ultimately, this configuration is ideal for a proactive "Roamer" or "Tank-Jungler" who needs to be everywhere at once while still being a legitimate threat to the enemy's backline.

equips.equip_desc: This build is designed for an aggressive playmaker who excels at diving team fights and surviving high-pressure encounters. By prioritizing Cooldown Reduction through Fleeting Time, you ensure your most impactful crowd-control skill is available for every engagement, while the combination of Queen's Wings and Oracle provides massive damage reduction and enhanced healing when your HP drops low. The inclusion of Brute Force Breastplate keeps you mobile and sturdy as you cast skills, while the Dire Hit blessing on your boots adds a surprising burst of damage to low-HP enemies. Ultimately, this setup transforms a standard tank into a persistent threat that can initiate frequently and survive extreme focus-fire.

hero.hero_overview: Marcel is a powerful Roam hero in Mobile Legends who excels at initiating fights and controlling enemies using strong crowd control skills. He is best used to set up team fights, protect allies, and create openings for damage dealers. With his burst capabilities and mobility, Marcel can quickly engage enemies and disrupt their positioning, making him a key asset in coordinated gameplay.
hero.hero_strength: Marcel is very effective in team fights due to his strong crowd control and initiation skills. He can easily catch enemies off guard and lock them down, giving his team a big advantage. In the early game, he can roam efficiently and help secure kills in different lanes. His burst damage also allows him to eliminate squishy targets quickly. Overall, he is strong in both engaging and supporting roles.
hero.hero_weakness: Marcel can be vulnerable when his skills are on cooldown, making him less effective in prolonged fights. He relies heavily on proper timing and positioning, so mistakes can lead to quick defeats. In the late game, he may struggle against high-damage or well-protected enemies. Additionally, he is not very durable compared to tank heroes, so poor engagements can easily result in being eliminated.

spell.spell_desc:
I chose Flicker for Marcel because it gives extra mobility, which is very important for a Roam hero. It allows Marcel to quickly engage enemies, escape dangerous situations, or reposition during team fights.
A good technique is to use Flicker together with his crowd control skills to surprise enemies and catch them off guard. You can also use it to initiate fights by blinking into the enemy backline or to save yourself when your skills are on cooldown. Timing is very important, so use Flicker wisely during crucial moments in clashes.

data.title: marcel tutorial

cooperates.cooperate_desc: She is perhaps Marcel's best teammate. When Marcel uses his ultimate, Golden Hour, it creates a "time-stop" zone that pauses all incoming projectiles. This allows Chang'e to fire her entire ultimate, Meteor Shower, into the zone. Instead of the enemy dodging the daggers, the projectiles stack up and hit the target all at once the moment the freeze ends, resulting in an instant "one-shot" burst.

Similar to Chang'e, Xavier's long-range spells benefit from Marcel's setup. Marcel can freeze an enemy in place, giving Xavier the perfect window to land his Dawning Light ultimate from across the map. Because the target is stuck in stasis, they have zero chance of using Flicker or a dash skill to escape Xavier's high-damage beam.

This is a "wombat-combo" dream. Tigreal can pull the enemy team together with his ultimate, and just as the stun is about to end, Marcel can drop his "Golden Hour" to keep them frozen for several more seconds. This extended lockdown gives your team's damage dealers more than enough time to reposition and wipe the entire enemy squad.

counters.counter_desc: She is a significant threat to Marcel because of her unique three-layered HP bar. Marcel relies on calculated bursts and passive true damage, but Masha's passive grants her a brief moment of invincibility every time one of her HP bars is depleted. This makes her almost impossible to "one-shot" with a timed photo-burst or a stasis combo.

She is a direct mechanical counter to Marcel's core utility. Her shield, Guardian's Reflection, has the power to block and reflect incoming projectiles. Since many of Marcel's skills—and the projectiles he tries to "freeze" for his teammates—count as shots, Lolita can deploy her shield to intercept them. If Marcel is not careful with his timing, he might find his own "frozen" skills or his teammates' projectiles being reflected right back at him, turning his best offensive tools into a deadly disadvantage.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Marcel truly starts to shine once he and his teammates have unlocked their Ultimates. During Turtle or Lord contests, Marcel is a game-changer because his ability to freeze units can stop the enemy jungler from using Retribution at the right time, or completely stall an enemy's engage. In these mid-game team fights, your goal is to stay close to your damage dealers and use your camera to provide constant vision and shields.
Once the team fight breaks out, look for the "Golden Hour." Use Marcel's Ultimate to freeze high-threat projectiles or lock down multiple enemies while your team repositioned for a counter-attack. Because you have almost no HP growth, you shouldn't be the one taking the first hit; instead, wait for your team to be ready and then use your utility to turn the tide of the battle. When everyone has their Ultimates ready, Marcel's presence makes the team nearly impossible to dive, allowing your carries to dish out damage safely.
dominants.dominant_title: Dominant Phase: Mid-Game Skirmishes & Turtle Fights

emblems.emblem_desc: The Support Emblem is the perfect choice for this build, as it provides a massive boost to Movement Speed, Hybrid Recovery, and Cooldown Reduction. These stats are essential for Marcel's roaming playstyle, allowing him to navigate the map quickly and use his time-manipulation skills as often as possible. Agility is taken in the first tier to further increase his base movement speed, making his Tracking Shot even more effective for catching up to enemies for a "photo" or escaping dangerous situations. Wilderness Blessing is selected for the second tier, granting him a significant speed advantage in the river and jungle areas, which is vital for securing early-game vision and rotations. Finally, Focusing Mark is the ideal finishing talent; since Marcel's camera companion automatically marks and damages nearby enemies, this talent ensures that your teammates deal increased damage to those targets, turning every snapshot into a potential kill for your team.

equips.equip_desc:
This build turns Marcel into a relentless, high-mobility disruption machine. Since he has poor HP growth, you must prioritize Dominance Ice (starting with the Steel Legplates component) to survive physical threats and reduce enemy healing. Your top priority is rushing Thunder Belt as early as possible; the sooner you acquire it, the more permanent defense stacks you can farm with your basic attacks.
Magic Boots with the Encourage blessing provide necessary cooldown reduction and team buffs, while Oracle and Radiant Armor ensure you can survive magic burst and amplify your own shields. Finally, Chastise Pauldron adds an extra layer of utility by reducing the damage dealt by nearby enemies, making you even harder to take down during team fights.

This item set is built to turn Marcel into a durable disruption machine. Since he has poor HP scaling, you must focus on building Dominance Ice (at least the Steel Legplates component) partially first to handle physical threats. However, your top priority is rushing Thunder Belt as early as possible; the sooner you get it, the more permanent defense stacks you can farm with your basic attacks. Tough Boots with Encourage help with rotations and CC, while Oracle and Brute Force Breastplate amplify your shields and movement speed. Finally, Radiant Armor provides the magic defense needed to survive late-game mages.

hero.hero_overview: Marcel is a revolutionary Support/Roamer known as the Soul Photographer. Unlike traditional tanks who rely on raw health, Marcel focuses on tempo disruption and vision. His kit revolves around his camera companion, Clemar, who can freeze enemies, projectiles, and even the Lord in their tracks. While he has almost no HP growth, he makes up for it with a unique passive that converts extra HP into Hybrid Defense, making him a "glass-cannon" tank that is all about timing and positioning rather than just soaking up damage.
hero.hero_strength:
First-of-its-kind Stasis: His ultimate, Golden Hour, is the only skill in the game that can freeze projectiles and units, effectively stopping an enemy's engage or save an ally from a killing blow.

Passive True Damage: His camera automatically snaps photos of nearby enemies, dealing True Damage based on their Max HP. This makes him surprisingly deadly against tanky opponents.
hero.hero_weakness:
Poor HP Scaling: Marcel doesn't gain much health from items or leveling. He is very vulnerable to True Damage (like Karrie or Lesley) and Magic Penetration since his actual health pool remains low.

Indiscriminate Ultimate: His ultimate freezes everyone—including his own teammates. If timed poorly, you can accidentally save an enemy or ruin your teammate's big play.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: These are his top partners. Their continuous projectile Ultimates (Meteor Shower/Chemical Refinement) get "stuck" in Marcel's time-freeze bubble. When the freeze ends, all the stored damage hits the enemy simultaneously, resulting in an instant kill.

Kimmy is a top-tier partner for Marcel because of his Time Freeze (Ultimate) mechanic. Since Marcel's Ultimate suspends all projectiles in mid-air, Kimmy can fire her entire Chemical Refinement (Ultimate) or her continuous basic attacks directly into the frozen zone.

counters.counter_desc: Valentina is the hardest counter to Marcel. By stealing Marcel Ultimate, she can cast her own Time Freeze back at his team. Since Marcel relies on the "stored damage" mechanic, a Valentina who times her stolen Ultimate correctly can freeze his teammates and stop the very projectiles Marcel was trying to use against her.

Marcel passive gives him a specialized "Armor" bar that makes him very tanky against traditional Physical and Magic damage. However, True Damage completely ignores this mechanic. Lesley and Karrie can shred through his HP bars much faster than other Marksmen, making his defensive stats feel useless.

While Marcel freeze is a "time" effect, Diggie Ultimate provides a massive shield and CC immunity to his team. If Diggie ults just as Marcel freeze ends, the burst of stored damage is often absorbed by the shield, effectively neutralizing Marcel big play.

dominants.dominant_desc:
- Laning: Stay near minions to use Thunder Belt stacks, poke enemies with basic attacks to trigger Concussive Blast, and avoid overextending without vision.

- Team Fights: Position at the front to protect allies, use crowd control abilities to disrupt enemies, and activate Oracle's effect when taking heavy damage or healing.

emblems.emblem_desc:
- Agility: This minor talent boosts movement speed, which is vital for Marcel as a roamer. It lets him rotate faster between lanes to assist teammates, secure objectives like turtles or lords, and reposition quickly during fights—critical for maximizing his utility by applying crowd control (from skills like Framed Moment) at the right moments.

- Tenacity: This minor talent reduces damage taken when Marcel HP drops below 40%. As a frontline support who often engages first or soaks up damage for allies, this helps him survive dangerous situations longer, ensuring he can still use his ultimate Golden Hour to turn the tide of battle or protect key teammates.

- Concussive Blast: The major talent deals area magic damage (100 + 0.07 Total HP) after a basic attack. Marcel kit involves close-quarters engagement, so this extra damage aids in clearing minion waves efficiently (speeding up lane pressure), harassing enemies in the laning phase, and adding consistent burst to his fight rotations. Since it scales with his total HP (a core stat for his tank build), it becomes more impactful as he stacks defensive items.

equips.equip_desc:
Tough Boots (Encourage): Provides early Magic Defense and Resilience while the Encourage blessing boosts nearby allies' attributes.

Thunder Belt: The most critical item for Marcel. It provides Mana Regen, HP, and the unique ability to stack Hybrid Defense through basic attacks, which fuels his damage scaling.

Dominance Ice: Essential if the enemy team has high healing or shield heroes (like Esmeralda or healers). It also provides a significant amount of Physical Defense for his skill scaling.

Oracle: Enhances the shields generated by Marcel's passive and provides Cooldown Reduction and Magic Defense.
Antique Cuirass: Best against Physical skill-based heroes (like Lapu-Lapu) to reduce their attack power.

Radiant Armor: Choose Radiant Armor for continuous magic damage (like from Chang'e or Valir).

hero.hero_overview: Marcel is MLBB new Support/Control hero (with Tank/Initiator flexibility), themed as a photographer wielding his camera "Clemar" to manipulate battlefield dynamics. His kit centers on crowd control, ally protection, and disrupting enemy formations. Farming/Roaming Route: Prioritize mid-lane assistance at level 2, then rotate to secure gold/exp lane objectives or invade enemy jungle with the jungler. Avoid solo farming—focus on enabling teammates and controlling map vision.
hero.hero_strength:
- Team Fight Dominance: His Ultimate can freeze multiple enemies and projectiles for 3 seconds, creating decisive windows for allies to deal damage or secure objectives like Lord/Turtle.

- Early-Mid Game Pressure: Passive grants reliable shielding and true damage, while Skill 2 high mobility lets him roam quickly to gank lanes or defend turrets.

- Unique Utility: Ability to halt enemy ultimates and skill shots (e.g., Natalya Ultimate, Lesley snipe) makes him a strong counter to burst or projectile-reliant heroes.

- Hybrid Survivability: HP converts to both physical and magic defense, letting him tank damage even with support-focused builds.
hero.hero_weakness:
- Skill Delays: Skill 1 and Ultimate have wind-up times—poor positioning or mistimed casts leave him vulnerable and reduce effectiveness.

- Low Sustained Damage: Relies entirely on teammates to capitalize on his CC; he cannot carry fights alone.

- Late-Game Limitations: While still useful for CC, his damage and shielding scale slower than dedicated tanks or enchanters, making him less impactful if the game drags on.

- Vulnerable to Melee Burst: Assassins or fighters with gap-closing abilities (e.g., Lancelot, Yu Zhong) can bypass his CC and eliminate him before he can react.

spell.spell_desc:
Marcel core strength lies in precise positioning to land his crowd control abilities, especially his Ultimate (Golden Hour) which has a fixed cast range and wind-up time. Flicker solves his main limitation of limited positioning flexibility by letting him reposition mid-cast or extend the reach of his skills. It also helps him escape sudden assassin ganks or close gaps to lock down high-priority enemies.
 
Techniques & Tips for Use:
 
1. Ultimate Extension: Start channeling Golden Hour, then use Flicker just before it activates to shift the center of the area of effect—this catches enemies off-guard who try to dodge by moving away from your initial position.

2. Skill 1 Combo: After casting Skill 1 (Framed Moment) to slow enemies, use Flicker to close in and ensure the immobilize effect lands on key targets, or to reposition safely if enemies counter-engage.

3. Escape/Retreat: If assassins or burst heroes target you, use Flicker to dodge their gap-closing skills (e.g., Lancelot Puncture) while activating Skill 2 (Tracking Shot) for extra movement speed to disengage.

4. Objective Contests: During Lord/Turtle fights, use Flicker to quickly move into position and deploy your Ultimate to freeze enemies trying to steal or contest the objective.

hero.hero_overview:
Marcel is a Support/Roam hero known as the Soul Photographer specializing in crowd control shielding and disruption Using his camera Clemar he can freeze enemies create time stop zones and support allies making him a strong frontline utility hero.

His passive, Platinum Snap makes his Basic Attacks scale with his defenses and offense while converting extra HP into Hybrid Defense Clemar also deals True Damage based on enemy HP and grants shields to Marcel and an ally encouraging sustained fights.

His first skill, Framed Moment slows enemies then immobilizes and deals scaling damage after a delay making it a strong setup tool.

His second skill, Tracking Shot boosts movement speed and allows a dash helping him chase or reposition while maintaining pressure and control

His ultimate Golden Hour creates a zone that freezes enemies and projectiles while allowing Marcel to move freely giving him strong teamfight control and resetting his passive for more damage and shielding.

Released Date: March 11 2026
Role Support: Tank
Specialty: Crowd/Control Utility
Lane Recommendation:  Roam
Damage Type: Physical

dominants.dominant_desc: To master Marcel, focus on Formation Disruption by using the massive speed from Tracking Shot (Skill 2) to dive the backline and trigger Framed Moment (Skill 1) for area-of-effect immobilization. His most critical tactic is the Strategic Stasis of his ultimate, Golden Hour, which should be used to pause high-damage projectiles, secure objectives like the Lord, or isolate specific enemies—though you must time it carefully to avoid freezing your own teammates during their skill casts. hero.hero_overview: Marcel is a new, specialized Support hero in MLBB (released March 2026) known as the "Soul Photographer". He features a unique, high-skill "freeze" mechanic that pauses enemy movement and projectiles, allowing for major tactical, team-fight-resetting plays. He relies on crowd control, with a passive that freezes enemies, a skill 1 stun/immobilize, and a skill 2 dash for mobility.
hero.hero_strength:
Key Strengths of Marcel:
Time-Stopping Ultimate (Golden Hour): Marcel's ultimate freezes all units (enemies, teammates, and summons) within a designated area for 3 seconds. This zone also suspends incoming projectiles and basic attacks, dealing reduced damage once the effect ends, allowing for massive teamfight control, objective securing (e.g., stopping the Lord), and saving allies. Unique Crowd Control & Passive (Platinum Snap): Marcel's passive, aided by his partner Clemar, deals True Damage based on the enemy's Max HP and immobilizes them. This passive also provides a shield to Marcel and one nearby ally. The Frozen Moment CC from his kit can even affect heroes with CC Immunity. High Defensive Scaling: Marcel does not benefit from attack speed; instead, his passive converts extra attack speed and critical damage into defense. Furthermore, a portion of his extra HP is converted into Hybrid Defense, making him naturally tanky and capable of building defensive items to enhance his damage. High Mobility (Tracking Shot): His second skill provides a significant movement speed boost (15%-27.5% base, up to 112.5% towards enemies), which can be used to chase down targets or escape. Area of Effect (AoE) Disruption (Framed Moment): His first skill slows enemies and immobilizes them after a 2.5-second delay, ideal for setting up combos in teamfights.
hero.hero_weakness:
Core Weaknesses of Marcel:
High Troll Potential: His ultimate, Golden Hour, freezes everyone in the area—including teammates. Misplacing it can "save" an enemy from a killing blow or trap an ally in a dangerous position where they are a sitting target once the effect ends. Vulnerability to True Damage: Marcel cannot benefit from extra HP; instead, he converts it into Hybrid Defense. This makes him extremely weak against heroes like Lesley or Karrie, who deal True Damage that ignores his high defense stats.
Low Early-Game Presence: He is considered a late-game scaler with a low initial HP pool. Aggressive early-game "hit and run" tactics can easily shut him down before he builds enough defense. Ult Interference with Allies: Marcel's ultimate can cancel or delay powerful allied channeling skills (like those of Odette, Ixia, or Gord). Teammates must perfectly time their skills to land after the freeze ends. Countered by "Outside" Damage: Heroes who can stay outside his ultimate's range and continue to fire projectiles into it (like Chang'e, Xavier, or Beatrix) can "stack" damage that hits instantly the moment the freeze ends.
Lacks Solo Kill Potential: He is a pure utility support and provides very little damage on his own. Without a coordinated team to follow up on his CC, he is largely ineffective and easy to pick off in a 1v1.

data.title: Marcel Pro Tips, Build and Emblem

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Heroes such as Change, Granger, and Kimmy are the absolute best partners for Marcel. Because his ultimate ability suspends all projectiles in mid-air and releases them all at once when the effect ends, these heroes can stack their entire damage output onto a frozen target. When the stasis breaks, the enemy takes a massive and unavoidable burst of damage instantly.

counters.counter_desc: Valentina is perhaps the most dangerous individual counter because she can steal Marcel ultimate. Since she becomes immune to the freeze while in his form, she can essentially use his own power against his team. This creates a situation where your own protector becomes the person who freezes you in place.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Mid to Late Game Strategy
During this phase, you should transition from being a passive protector to an aggressive playmaker. Your goal is to control the map and force the enemy team to deal with your presence while your damage dealers focus on objectives.
Laning and Movement Precautions
Avoid Overextending Alone: Even though you are very durable, you do not have the damage to finish off enemies by yourself. You should always ensure a teammate is nearby before you commit to a heavy engagement.

Vision Control: Use your high health to check bushes and dark areas of the map. It is much safer for you to face-check a hidden enemy than for your marksman or mage to do so.
Combat and Fight Strategies
The Conceal Surprise: Use the Conceal active on your boots to hide your teams movement. This is most effective when you are rotating through the river to catch the enemy midlaner or marksman out of position.

The Flicker and Ultimate Combo: Since your ultimate has a slight casting delay, you can begin the animation and then use Flicker to teleport into the middle of the enemy formation. This gives the opponents almost no time to react or move out of the area of effect.

emblems.emblem_desc:
This emblem setup is all about turning Marcel into an unkillable frontline sponge. The Tank Emblem gives him the raw health and defense he needs to survive long enough to actually use his crowd control.
Tenacity is the key here, it kicks in when his health gets low, giving him a massive defense boost that often catches enemies off guard when they think they have almost killed him. Pair that with Concussive Blast, and he is not just soaking up damage he is also dealing a decent amount of area-of-effect magic damage just by being near his targets. It basically rewards him for staying in the thick of the fight.

equips.equip_desc:
The reason I suggested the Tank Emblem for your specific build—Tough Boots with Conceal, Dominance Ice, Radiant Armor, the new Chestplate, Oracle, and Guardian Helmet—is because it turns you into an unstoppable regeneration machine.

Since your equipment choices focus heavily on Health Points and Health Regeneration, the Tank Emblem is the best fit because its base stats provide the highest possible boost to your total health. This directly improves the effectiveness of the Guardian Helmet and Oracle, as their healing properties are more powerful when you have a larger health pool.

The Tenacity talent is also vital because it grants you extra physical and magic defense when your health drops below forty percent. This gives your passive healing from the Oracle and Radiant Armor the time they need to work, preventing you from being defeated by a single burst of damage. Finally, Concussive Blast is the perfect choice because it deals magic damage based on your maximum health. Since your build is packed with high-health items, you will actually deal a surprising amount of damage just by standing near your enemies.

Essentially, this combination ensures that you are not just a passive target, but a durable protector who can sustain through long battles and punish anyone who gets too close.

hero.hero_overview: arcel is a heavy-hitting tank who excels at disrupting the enemy line and soaking up massive amounts of damage. His kit is built around high survivability and crowd control, making him a nightmare for squishies who get caught in his reach. While he might lack the raw mobility of an assassin, his ability to lock down targets and peel for teammates makes him an essential anchor for any balanced team composition.
hero.hero_strength: Marcel is built to be an absolute sponge, soaking up massive amounts of damage that would normally wipe out his teammates. His main strength is his crowd control, which lets him physically disrupt the enemy and peel for his carries during chaotic fights. Because he scales so well with defensive items, he becomes nearly unkillable as the game goes on, forcing the other team to waste their best abilities just to slow him down.
hero.hero_weakness: Marcel biggest drawback is that he cannot really carry a game on his own. he is completely dependent on his teammates to follow up when he jumps in. If his team isn't coordinated, his ultimate can actually backfire by accidentally freezing allies or saving enemies from certain death at the wrong time. He also struggles against true damage and high-penetration heroes because he doesnot benefit from extra HP, making him feel surprisingly squishy in the late game. and once he burns his long cooldowns, he basically becomes a sitting duck with very little impact until his skills reset.

spell.spell_desc: Flicker is a battle spell that allows you to instantly teleport a short distance in a chosen direction. After the blink, you gain a small boost to your Physical and Magical Defense for 1 second, which helps with survivability right after repositioning. It is primarily used to either escape a dangerous situation or to catch enemies off guard by closing the gap instantly. Most players rely on it because it has a long cooldown, so timing is everything.
